title: The executive presidency must go. The only question is how. Realistically, the only option is to push for the best possible 21st Amendment as an interim measure and as a bridge to a new constitution.
body_lines: Sri Lanka: 2019, a lower middle income country; 2020, a bankrupted, beggared land, dependent on international charity for survival.

“Poor politics has shattered my life and this is my only hope to put food on the table for my children.” The speaker is a 33-year-old Sri Lankan woman who was forced by the unravelling economy to become a sex worker (The Telegraph – 28.5.2022).

Poor politics resulting in bad economics – that was what caused Sri Lanka’s dizzying slide from relative success to absolute failure. At a recent COPE meeting, Central Bank officials and Monetary Board members talked of the massive tax cut and the wasting of billions in foreign reserves to maintain the rupee at 203 to a dollar. Add mass money printing and an abracadabra-style fertiliser policy and you have it: How to ruin a country in four steps.

Dr. Ranee Jayamaha of the Monetary Board informed COPE that the IMF refused to give Sri Lanka a loan for balance of payment support under its Rapid Financing Instrument citing debt unsustainability. That was in the first half of 2020. That warning was swept under the carpet. The Rajapaksas, like the fox in the sour grapes story, cried, ‘Who needs the IMF!’

Why go over that old ground? The house is burnt, gutted; we should focus on putting up a basic shelter to shield ourselves from the worst of the storms. That is the Rajapaksa argument. But unless we understand why the house caught fire, how can we take measures to protect the new shelter from a similar calamity?

Bad politics breed bad economics. One family landed Sri Lanka in this mess. In February 2022, the pharmacy owners association warned that current medicine stocks were enough only for three months. By that time the Central Bank has already sold half of our gold reserves. The Government was too busy to take note. In February, Namal Rajapaksa inaugurated a water sports centre at the Colombo Port City “offering a range of exhilarating sea-borne experiences…aimed at filling an unmet need.” In the same month the President inaugurated the Dharmapala Park in Galle, renovated at Rs. 200 million, under his Strategic Cities Development Program. Even as late as end March, President Gotabaya was planning another visit to a State institution. A letter was issued to the staff of the Central Environment Authority telling them to get the COVID-19 booster shot and undergo an antigen test. The visit, scheduled for April Fool’s Day, was prevented only by the Mirihana protest.

The concentration of power which enabled the Rajapaksa clan to bankrupt Sri Lanka must be ended. We must ensure that no one individual (with or without his family) can block our path to recovery and hurl us into ruin again.

Thus the importance of the 21st Amendment. Good economics cannot be without good politics; a new policy framework to raise revenue, cut costs, curb inflations, create an adequate safety net for the old and new poor must go hand in hand with the 21st Amendment.

The Rajapaksas never left. They merely retreated into the woodwork until public anger subsided. Now they are back. Basil Rajapaksa is as busy as ever, pulling strings. His main concern is to keep formal power in the hands of the family. To do that, he must prevent the 21st Amendment.

The argument that economics must be given primacy over politics is a specious one, concocted in service of Rajapaksa power. The Rajapaksas who gutted the economy are now using the resultant pain to hang on to power. It is obscene but it may succeed, if the Opposition doesn’t get together to push the 21st Amendment through.

If we did not have a presidential system, one man and his family would not have been able to gut the economy with such ease and impunity. If we didn’t have a presidential system, the leader who set fire to the economy would be out of a job now. The executive presidency must go. The only question is how. Do we reject an amendment to reduce presidential powers immediately and hold out for a new constitution? Or do we go for a two-step solution, a 21st Amendment to be followed by a new constitution?

The Rajapaksas know the truth the Opposition is refusing to acknowledge. A 21st Amendment is near-fatal to the family’s dream of making a comeback soon. That is why Basil – and probably Namal – Rajapaksa is rallying the SLPP to delay and destroy the 21st Amendment. If they succeed, there’ll be neither a 21st Amendment nor a new constitution.

A parliamentary election is necessary, but practically impossible in current conditions. The absolute majority of people are caught up in the daily battle for survival, battered by shortages, pulverised by inflation. An election campaign will be a parliamentary session re-enacted on a national stage for five or more weeks. While ordinary people suffer in hunger and want, politicians will be wasting scarce resources on propaganda. That spectacle is likely to cause not an eagerness to vote, but to gut the parliament, metaphorically and literally. An election might act as a spark to ignite public anger against the entire democratic system.

At best, an immediate election will have a low turnout; at worst we will have hungry and despairing people attacking politicians and their meetings. Such a situation will play into Rajapaksa hands since the election will be held with the 20th Amendment extant and presidential powers untrimmed. If expressions of public ire border on anarchy, it might even open the door to military intervention with or without the Rajapaksas.
Realistically, the only option is to push for the best possible 21st Amendment as an interim measure and as a bridge to a new constitution.

The beggaring of Sri Lanka has caused the de-sanctification of the Rajapaksas. Mahinda and Gotabaya Rajapaksa have lost their halos. The family is weak, and possibly politically divided. Until the events of 9 May, there was certain symmetry in the varying ambitions of different Rajapaksas, giving them an objective reason to work together as a family. Now there is no symmetry between their varying interests.

The Opposition can use this divergence of interests to undermine Basil Rajapaksa’s hold on the SLPP, and through that Rajapaksa power in Parliament (that power was on display during the election for a deputy speaker). If handled correctly, the 21st Amendment can be used to split the SLPP, giving a way out for those who still have some form of vertebrae to save themselves from a lifetime of slavering for Basil and Namal Rajapaksa.

If the SLPP splits, that might persuade Gotabaya Rajapaksa to look out solely for himself. After all, his best interests, as distinct from his family’s, lie in promoting the 21st Amendment and backing the abolition of the presidency. The Rajapaksa family has nothing to lose and a world to regain. Gotabaya Rajapaksa has something to lose, an orderly departure from presidency. He also has something to gain, a grain of redemption as the executive president who finally abolished the executive presidency.

The recent deplorable arrests of leading Galle Face protestors point to the danger of renewed repression. The 20th Amendment re-politicised the police, enabling SLPP parliamentarians to appoint the area OICs of their choice, as was recently revealed. Such a system is particularly conducive to a new wave of repression. A 21st Amendment will act as a bar to the abuse of police powers and further strengthen the judiciary. An election held under the 21st Amendment will be more free and fair, and thus of benefit to all Opposition parties. Incidentally, an election and a referendum on the presidency can he held together. Going by the latest CPA opinion poll, most Lankans would vote to abolish the presidency. The incoming parliament can then turn itself into a constitutional council and draft a new democratic constitution.

In his comments on the lessons of 20th Century, Italian historian and anti-fascist fighter Leo Valiani said, “Our century demonstrates that the victory of the ideals of justice is equality is always ephemeral, but also that, if we manage to preserve liberty, we can always start all over again…” (quoted in The Age of Extremes – Eric Hobsbwam). The 21st Amendment is the essential first step in saving Lankan democracy both from a Rajapaksa power grab and a military intervention.

The JVP has at least come up with their conditions for supporting the 21st Amendment. The SJB and other Opposition parties are yet to do even that. The battle for the 21st Amendment is for Opposition’s to lose. Will the Opposition hold out for the ideal, thereby preventing the possible and enabling the very worst to make a triumphant return?

Let’s face reality. Without India, Sri Lanka would have come to a standstill literally weeks ago. If India doesn’t grant us the new loan we are seeking, we may come to a standstill yet. This is not the time to embrace politics of anti-Indianism. It is impolite, and self-destructively stupid. If India washes her hands off us, can we eat Kachchativu or the Trinco oil tank farm?

Similar help from China is unlikely for China is scaling back, having overstretched itself. Pacific and not the Indian Ocean is Beijing’s new focus. The West is focused on Ukraine. India and to a lesser extent Japan are our only hope.

There is something we can learn from China’s own economic troubles. According to Michael Pettis, a professor at Beijing University, President Xi has recently warned about the difference between genuine and inflated GDP growth: “Broadly speaking, genuine growth can be thought of as sustainable growth generated largely by consumption, exports, and business investment (with the last of these elements aimed mostly at serving the first two) whereas ‘inflated’ growth consist mainly of non-productive or insufficiently productive, investment in infrastructure and real estate” (The only five paths China’s economy can follow).

Physical infrastructure development and stock market were what passed for growth and development in Sri Lanka for a long time. We piled up debt, with very little to show for it in terms of actual benefits to real people or lasting economic improvement.

Sri Lanka’s poverty rate is probably over the 50% by now. As Steve Hanks of John Hopkins University pointed out, “Inflation is crushing the poorest…” (Al Jazeera – 28.5.2022). Retrenchment will drive up unemployment, pushing entire families into poverty. According to the Stand Up Movement Lanka (SUML), an advocacy group for sex workers, “There has been a 30 per cent increase in women joining the sex industry in Colombo since January” (The Telegraph – 28.5.2022). The numbers can only increase. The Thriposha factory has been closed; its impact on child malnutrition levels will be devastating. There will be more babies born underweight, more children growing stunted, more students dropping out of school, more child and maternal deaths, more destitute elderly. Social achievements of seven decades will be wiped out. Several generations will be lost, thanks to the imbecilic power hunger of one family.

Much will depend on the new economic policy framework and the budget Prime Minister Wickremesinghe has promised. Will the Government stop pulverising the poor and raise revenue by increasing personal and corporate income taxes and introducing a wealth tax targeting our own 1%? Will military expenditure and capital expenditure be pruned and resultant savings used to protect the poor from starvation? Will there be focus on solar power and on improving public transport, as a way of alleviating the power and the fuel crises?
Or will there be just talk, and no action leading to a vicious cycle of oppressive economic policies and violent protests, State violence and popular violence?

Two examples, one new and the other old. BPPL Holdings PLC, a Lankan company which collects used plastic bottles and turns them into recycled polyester for international sports brands, was awarded a $ 15 million loan from the United States International Development Finance Corporation. While Sri Lanka’s failed state is hard pressed to obtain even a loan of a million dollars, innovative private and social enterprises can still access international grants and loans.

When Ranasinghe Premadasa came up with the idea for a free midday meal program for schoolchildren, the cabinet of President Jayewardene turned it down due to lack of funds. The country was indeed in a politico-economic mire. But child malnutrition too was becoming rampant. PM Premadasa and Mayor Sirisena Cooray came up with the idea of implementing the program in Colombo, where around 50% of the people lived below or close to the poverty line. To find the funds, Sirisena Cooray replaced the CMC’s usual practice of increasing rates every five years by a large sum with an annual and much smaller rate increase. This program became the precursor of the free midday meal program implemented nationally during the Premadasa presidency.

Accept reality, think creatively, and don’t let better be the enemy of good. We need the 21st Amendment, higher taxes on the rich, and a safety net to stop the old and new poor from being driven to violence. Both together; both now.

si.title: විධායක ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමය යා යුතුයි. එකම ප්රශ්නය වන්නේ කෙසේද යන්නයි. යථාර්තවාදීව, එකම විකල්පය නම්, හැකි හොඳම 21 වැනි සංශෝධනය අතුරු පියවරක් ලෙස සහ නව ව්‍යවස්ථාවකට පාලමක් ලෙස ගෙන ඒමයි.
body_lines: ශ්‍රී ලංකාව: 2019, අඩු මැදි ආදායම් රටක්; 2020, පැවැත්ම සඳහා ජාත්‍යන්තර පුණ්‍යායතන මත යැපෙන බංකොලොත්, හිඟමන ඉඩමක්.

"දුප්පත් දේශපාලනය මගේ ජීවිතය සුනුවිසුනු කර ඇති අතර මගේ දරුවන්ට කෑම මේසය මත තැබීම මගේ එකම බලාපොරොත්තුව මෙයයි." කථිකයා 33 හැවිරිදි ශ්‍රී ලාංකික කාන්තාවක් වන අතර ඇය ලිහිල් ආර්ථිකය විසින් ලිංගික ශ්‍රමිකයෙකු වීමට බල කරන ලදී (The Telegraph - 28.5.2022).

දුර්වල දේශපාලනයේ ප්‍රතිඵලයක් ලෙස අයහපත් ආර්ථිකය - එය සාපේක්ෂ සාර්ථකත්වයේ සිට පරම අසාර්ථකත්වය දක්වා ශ්‍රී ලංකාව කරකැවිල්ලට තල්ලු වීමට හේතු විය. පසුගියදා පැවති කෝප් කමිටු රැස්වීමේදී මහ බැංකු නිලධාරීන් සහ මුදල් මණ්ඩලයේ සාමාජිකයන් රුපියල ඩොලරයට 203ක් ලෙස පවත්වා ගැනීම සඳහා දැවැන්ත බදු කප්පාදුව සහ විදේශ සංචිත බිලියන ගණනින් නාස්ති කිරීම ගැන කතා කළහ. මහා මුදල් මුද්‍රණය සහ abracadabra ආකාරයේ පොහොර ප්‍රතිපත්තියක් එක් කරන්න, ඔබට එය තිබේ: පියවර හතරකින් රටක් විනාශ කරන්නේ කෙසේද.

මූල්‍ය මණ්ඩලයේ ආචාර්ය රනී ජයමහ කෝප් ආයතනයට දන්වා සිටියේ ණය තිරසාර නොවන බව පවසමින් IMF විසින් ශ්‍රී ලංකාවට සිය වේගවත් මූල්‍යකරණ උපකරණය යටතේ ගෙවුම් ශේෂ සහය සඳහා ණයක් ලබාදීම ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළ බවයි. ඒ 2020 මුල් භාගයේදීය. එම අනතුරු ඇඟවීම කාපට් යට ගසාගෙන ගියේය. ඇඹුල් මිදි කතාවේ නරියා මෙන් රාජපක්ෂලා ‘කාටද IMF ඕනෑ!’ කියා කෑ ගැසූහ.

ඇයි අර පරණ බිම උඩින් යන්නෙ? නිවස පුළුස්සා, ගිනිබත් කර ඇත; දරුණුතම කුණාටුවලින් ආරක්ෂා වීමට මූලික නවාතැනක් තැබීමට අප අවධානය යොමු කළ යුතුය. ඒක තමයි රාජපක්ෂ තර්කය. නමුත් නිවස ගිනිගත්තේ මන්දැයි අපට වැටහෙන්නේ නැත්නම්, නව නවාතැන එවැනි විපතකින් ආරක්ෂා කිරීමට පියවර ගන්නේ කෙසේද?

නරක දේශපාලනය නරක ආර්ථිකය ඇති කරයි. එක පවුලක් ලංකාව මේ අවුලෙන් ගොඩ දැම්මා. 2022 පෙබරවාරි මාසයේදී ඔසුසල් හිමියන්ගේ සංගමය අනතුරු ඇඟවූයේ දැනට පවතින ඖෂධ තොග මාස තුනකට පමණක් ප්‍රමාණවත් බවයි. ඒ වන විටත් මහ බැංකුව අපේ රන් සංචිතවලින් අඩක් විකුණා අවසන්. එය සැලකිල්ලට ගත නොහැකි තරමට රජය කාර්ය බහුල විය. පෙබරවාරි මාසයේදී නාමල් රාජපක්ෂ විසින් කොළඹ වරාය නගරයේ ජල ක්‍රීඩා මධ්‍යස්ථානයක් විවෘත කළේය. එම මාසයේදීම ජනාධිපතිතුමා විසින් ගාල්ල ධර්මපාල උද්‍යානය විවෘත කරන ලද අතර රුපියල් මිලියන 100 ක වියදමින් ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණය කරන ලදී. මිලියන 200, ඔහුගේ උපාය මාර්ගික නගර සංවර්ධන වැඩසටහන යටතේ. මාර්තු අග වන විටත් ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය තවත් රාජ්‍ය ආයතනයක සංචාරයක් සැලසුම් කරමින් සිටියේය. මධ්‍යම පරිසර අධිකාරියේ කාර්ය මණ්ඩලයට කොවිඩ් 19 බූස්ටර ෂොට් එක ලබාගෙන ප්‍රතිදේහජනක පරීක්ෂණයකට භාජනය කරන ලෙස දන්වා ලිපියක් නිකුත් කර ඇත. අප්‍රේල් මෝඩයාගේ දිනයට නියමිතව තිබූ එම සංචාරය වැළැක්වූයේ මිරිහාන විරෝධතාවයෙන් පමණි.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාව බංකොලොත් කිරීමට රාජපක්ෂ වංශයට හැකි වූ බල ඒකරාශී වීම අවසන් කළ යුතුය. කිසිවකුට (ඔහුගේ පවුල සමඟ හෝ නැතිව) අපගේ ප්‍රකෘතිමත් වීමේ මාවත අවහිර කර නැවත අපව විනාශයට ඇද දැමිය නොහැකි බවට අපි සහතික විය යුතුය.

මේ අනුව 21 වැනි සංශෝධනයේ වැදගත්කම. යහපත් ආර්ථිකයක් යහපත් දේශපාලනයක් නොමැතිව පැවතිය නොහැක. ආදායම් ඉහළ නැංවීමට, වියදම් කපා හැරීමට, උද්ධමනය මැඩලීමට, පැරණි සහ නව දුප්පතුන්ට ප්‍රමාණවත් ආරක්ෂිත දැලක් නිර්මාණය කිරීමට නව ප්‍රතිපත්ති රාමුවක් 21 වැනි සංශෝධනය සමඟ අත්වැල් බැඳගත යුතුය.

රාජපක්ෂලා කවදාවත් ගියේ නැහැ. මහජන කෝපය පහව යන තෙක් ඔවුන් හුදෙක් ලී වැඩවලට පසුබැස ගියහ. දැන් ඔවුන් ආපසු පැමිණ ඇත. බැසිල් රාජපක්ෂ වෙනදා මෙන්ම කඹ අදිමින් කාර්ය බහුලය. ඔහුගේ ප්‍රධාන සැලකිල්ල වන්නේ විධිමත් බලය පවුල අතේ තබා ගැනීමයි. ඒ සඳහා ඔහු 21 වැනි සංශෝධනය වැළැක්විය යුතුය.

දේශපාලනයට වඩා ආර්ථිකයට ප්‍රමුඛත්වය දිය යුතුය යන තර්කය රාජපක්ෂ බලයට සේවය කිරීම සඳහා ගොතන ලද සූක්ෂම එකකි. ආර්ථිකය ගිල දැමූ රාජපක්‍ෂලා දැන් බලයේ එල්ලී සිටීම සඳහා එහි ප්‍රතිඵලය වන වේදනාව පාවිච්චි කරති. එය අසභ්‍ය නමුත් 21 වැනි ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනය ගෙන ඒම සඳහා විපක්ෂය එකතු නොවන්නේ නම් එය සාර්ථක විය හැකිය.

අපට ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමයක් නොතිබුනේ නම්, එක් මිනිසෙකුට සහ ඔහුගේ පවුලට මෙතරම් පහසුවෙන් සහ දණ්ඩමුක්තියකින් ආර්ථිකය ගිල දැමීමට නොහැකි වනු ඇත. අපට ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමයක් නොතිබුණා නම් ආර්ථිකයට ගිනි තැබූ නායකයාට දැන් රැකියාවක් නැත. විධායක ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමය යා යුතුයි. එකම ප්රශ්නය වන්නේ කෙසේද යන්නයි. ජනාධිපති බලතල වහා අඩු කිරීමේ සංශෝධනයක් ප්‍රතික්‍ෂේප කර නව ව්‍යවස්ථාවක් වෙනුවෙන් පෙනී සිටිනවාද? එසේත් නැතිනම් අපි පියවර දෙකක විසඳුමකට යනවාද, නව ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවකින් අනුගමනය කළ යුතු 21 වැනි සංශෝධනයකටද?

විපක්ෂය පිළිගැනීම ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කරන ඇත්ත රාජපක්ෂලා දන්නවා. 21 වැනි සංශෝධනය ඉක්මනින් නැවත පැමිණීමේ පවුලේ සිහිනයට මාරාන්තික ය. 21 වැනි සංශෝධනය ප්‍රමාද කර විනාශ කිරීමට බැසිල් - බොහෝ විට නාමල් - රාජපක්ෂ ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණ පෙළගස්වන්නේ එබැවිනි. ඔවුන් සාර්ථක වුවහොත් 21 වැනි සංශෝධනයක් හෝ නව ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවක් හෝ ඇති නොවේ.

පාර්ලිමේන්තු මැතිවරණයක් අවශ්‍ය නමුත් පවතින තත්ත්වය අනුව ප්‍රායෝගිකව කළ නොහැක්කකි. නිරපේක්ෂ බහුතරයක් මිනිසුන් හිඟයෙන් බැටකමින්, උද්ධමනය මගින් කුඩු පට්ටම් කර, පැවැත්ම සඳහා දෛනික සටනට හසු වී සිටිති. මැතිවරණ ව්‍යාපාරයක් යනු සති පහක් හෝ ඊට වැඩි කාලයක් ජාතික වේදිකාවක නැවත ක්‍රියාත්මක වන පාර්ලිමේන්තු සැසියක් වේ. සාමාන්‍ය ජනතාව කුසගින්නෙන් හා අගහිඟකම්වලින් පෙළෙන අතරේ දේශපාලනඥයන් ප්‍රචාරණය සඳහා හිඟ සම්පත් නාස්ති කරනු ඇත. එම දර්ශනය ඡන්දය දීමට ඇති ආශාවක් නොව, රූපක සහ වචනාර්ථයෙන් පාර්ලිමේන්තුව ගිල දැමීමට හේතු වනු ඇත. සමස්ත ප‍්‍රජාතන්ත‍්‍රවාදී ක‍්‍රමයට එරෙහිව මහජන කෝපය ඇවිලවීමේ ගිනි පුපුරක් ලෙස මැතිවරණයක් ක‍්‍රියා කළ හැකිය.

හොඳම දෙය නම්, ක්ෂණික මැතිවරණයක් අඩු ඡන්දය ප්‍රකාශ කිරීමක් වනු ඇත; නරකම අවස්ථාවක අපට බඩගිනි සහ බලාපොරොත්තු සුන් වූ මිනිසුන් දේශපාලකයන්ට සහ ඔවුන්ගේ රැස්වීම්වලට පහර දෙනු ඇත. 20 වැනි ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනයෙන් ජනාධිපති බලතල කප්පාදු නොකර මැතිවරණය පැවැත්වෙන බැවින් එවැනි තත්ත්වයක් රාජපක්ෂ අතට පත් වනු ඇත. මහජන කෝපයේ ප්‍රකාශයන් අරාජිකත්වයට මායිම් වන්නේ නම්, එය රාජපක්ෂවරුන් සමඟ හෝ නැතිව මිලිටරි මැදිහත්වීමකට පවා දොර විවර කළ හැකිය.
යථාර්තවාදීව, එකම විකල්පය නම්, හැකි හොඳම 21 වැනි සංශෝධනය අතුරු පියවරක් ලෙස සහ නව ව්‍යවස්ථාවකට පාලමක් ලෙස ගෙන ඒමයි.

ලංකාවේ හිඟාකෑම රාජපක්ෂලාගේ විශුද්ධිකරණයට හේතු වී ඇත. මහින්දගේ සහ ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂගේ හැල හැලිලා. පවුල දුර්වලයි, සමහර විට දේශපාලනික වශයෙන් බෙදී ඇත. මැයි 9 වැනි දින සිදුවීම් දක්වා විවිධ රාජපක්ෂවරුන්ගේ විවිධ අභිලාෂයන් තුළ යම් සමමිතියක් පැවති අතර, ඔවුන් පවුලක් ලෙස එකට වැඩ කිරීමට වෛෂයික හේතුවක් ලබා දුන්නේය. දැන් ඔවුන්ගේ විවිධ රුචිකත්වයන් අතර සමමිතියක් නොමැත.

ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණේ බැසිල් රාජපක්ෂගේ ග්‍රහණය හෑල්ලු කිරීමට විපක්ෂයට මෙම අවශ්‍යතා වෙනස් කිරීම භාවිතා කළ හැකි අතර, ඒ හරහා පාර්ලිමේන්තුවේ රාජපක්ෂ බලය (නියෝජ්‍ය කථානායකවරයෙකු සඳහා වන මැතිවරණයේදී එම බලය ප්‍රදර්ශනය විය). නිවැරදිව හසුරුවන්නේ නම්, 21 වැනි සංශෝධනය ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණ දෙකඩ කිරීමට යොදා ගත හැකි අතර, තවමත් යම් ආකාරයක කශේරුකාවක් ඇති අයට බැසිල්ගේ සහ නාමල් රාජපක්ෂගේ ජීවිත කාලය පුරාම වහල්භාවයෙන් ගැලවීමට මගක් ලබා දිය හැකිය.

ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණ දෙකඩ වුවහොත් එය ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂට තමන් ගැන පමණක් සොයා බැලීමට පොළඹවනු ඇත. සියල්ලට පසු, ඔහුගේ හොඳම අවශ්‍යතා, ඔහුගේ පවුලේ අයට වඩා වෙනස් ලෙස, 21 වැනි සංශෝධනය ප්‍රවර්ධනය කිරීම සහ ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමය අහෝසි කිරීමට පිටුබලය දීමයි. රාජපක්‍ෂ පවුලට නැතිවෙන්න දෙයක් නෑ ආයෙත් ලෝකයක් නෑ. ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂට නැතිවීමට යමක් තිබේ, ජනාධිපති ධුරයෙන් පිළිවෙළකට ඉවත්වීම. අවසානයේ විධායක ජනාධිපති ක‍්‍රමය අහෝසි කළ විධායක ජනාධිපතිවරයා ලෙස ඔහුටද ලබා ගැනීමට යමක් තිබේ.

ගාලු මුවදොර ප්‍රමුඛ පෙළේ විරෝධතාකරුවන් මෑතක දී සිදු වූ ඛේදනීය අත් අඩංගුවට ගැනීම් පෙන්නුම් කරන්නේ යළිත් මර්දනයේ අන්තරායයි. 20 වැනි ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනය මගින් පොලිසිය යළි දේශපාලනීකරණය කරන ලද අතර, පසුගියදා අනාවරණය වූ පරිදි ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණේ පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රීවරුන්ට තමන් කැමති ප්‍රදේශයේ ස්ථානාධිපතිවරුන් පත් කිරීමට හැකි විය. එවැනි පද්ධතියක් නව මර්දන රැල්ලකට විශේෂයෙන් හිතකරය. 21 වැනි ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනයක් මඟින් පොලිස් බලතල අයුතු ලෙස භාවිත කිරීමට බාධාවක් වන අතර අධිකරණය තවදුරටත් ශක්තිමත් වනු ඇත. 21 වැනි ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනය යටතේ පැවැත්වෙන මැතිවරණයක් වඩාත් නිදහස් හා සාධාරණ වන අතර එමඟින් සියලු විරුද්ධ පක්ෂවලට වාසිදායක වනු ඇත. අහඹු ලෙස, ඔහුට මැතිවරණයක් සහ ජනාධිපති ධුරය පිළිබඳ ජනමත විචාරණයක් එකට පැවැත්විය හැකිය. නවතම CPA මත විමසුමට අනුව, බොහෝ ලාංකිකයන් ජනාධිපති ධූරය අහෝසි කිරීමට ඡන්දය දෙනු ඇත. පසුව එන පාර්ලිමේන්තුවට ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථා සභාවක් බවට පත් වී නව ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී ව්‍යවස්ථාවක් සම්පාදනය කළ හැකිය.

20 වැනි සියවසේ පාඩම් පිළිබඳ ඔහුගේ අදහස් දැක්වීමේදී ඉතාලි ඉතිහාසඥයෙකු සහ ෆැසිස්ට් විරෝධී සටන්කරුවෙකු වන ලියෝ වලියානි මෙසේ පැවසීය: "අපගේ සියවස පෙන්නුම් කරන්නේ යුක්තියේ පරමාදර්ශවල ජයග්‍රහණය සමානාත්මතාවය සැමවිටම අනිත්‍යයි, නමුත් අපි නිදහස ආරක්ෂා කිරීමට සමත් වුවහොත්, අපට සෑම විටම නැවත ආරම්භ කළ හැක..." (උපුටා ගැනීම අන්තවාදී යුගයේ - එරික් හොබ්ස්බ්වම්). 21 වැනි ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනය ශ්‍රී ලංකා ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදය රාජපක්ෂ බලය අල්ලා ගැනීමකින් සහ මිලිටරි මැදිහත්වීමකින් ගලවා ගැනීමේ අත්‍යවශ්‍ය පළමු පියවරයි.

ජවිපෙ අවම වශයෙන් 21 වැනි ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනයට සහාය දැක්වීම සඳහා ඔවුන්ගේ කොන්දේසි ඉදිරිපත් කර ඇත. SJB සහ අනෙකුත් විරුද්ධ පක්ෂ තවමත් එයවත් කර නැත. 21 වැනි සංශෝධනය සඳහා වන සටන විපක්ෂයේ පරාජය සඳහා ය. විපක්ෂය පරමාදර්ශය වෙනුවෙන් පෙනී සිටින අතර, එමඟින් හැකි දේ වළක්වා, නරකම දේට ජයග්‍රාහී නැවත පැමිණීමට හැකි වේවිද?

යථාර්ථයට මුහුණ දෙමු. ඉන්දියාව නොවන්නට ශ්‍රී ලංකාව වචනාර්ථයෙන් සති කිහිපයකට පෙර නැවතීමට ඉඩ තිබුණි. අප සොයන නව ණය ඉන්දියාව අපට ලබා නොදෙන්නේ නම්, අපි තවමත් නතර විය හැකිය. මෙය ඉන්දීය විරෝධී දේශපාලනය වැළඳ ගැනීමට කාලය නොවේ. එය අශික්ෂිත ය, ස්වයං-විනාශකාරී මෝඩ ය. ඉන්දියාව අපෙන් අත හෝදුවහොත් අපට කච්චතිව් හෝ ට්‍රින්කෝ තෙල් ටැංකි සංකීර්ණය කන්න පුළුවන්ද?

චීනය ඕනෑවට වඩා දිගු කර ඇති නිසා චීනයෙන් එවැනිම උපකාරයක් නොලැබේ. බීජිං හි නව අවධානය යොමු වන්නේ පැසිෆික් සහ ඉන්දියන් සාගරය නොවේ. බටහිර යුක්රේනය කෙරෙහි අවධානය යොමු කර ඇත. ඉන්දියාව සහ ජපානය අපේ එකම බලාපොරොත්තුවයි.

චීනයේ ආර්ථික ගැටලුවලින් අපට ඉගෙන ගත හැකි දෙයක් තිබේ. බීජිං විශ්ව විද්‍යාලයේ මහාචාර්යවරයෙකු වන මයිකල් පෙටිස් පවසන පරිදි, සැබෑ සහ උද්ධමනය වූ දළ දේශීය නිෂ්පාදිතයේ වර්ධනය අතර වෙනස ගැන ජනාධිපති ෂී මෑතකදී අනතුරු ඇඟවීය: “පුළුල් ලෙස කථා කරන විට, අව්‍යාජ වර්ධනය පරිභෝජනය, අපනයන සහ ව්‍යාපාරික ආයෝජන මගින් ජනනය වන තිරසාර වර්ධනයක් ලෙස සැලකිය හැකිය. මෙම මූලද්‍රව්‍යවලින් අවසාන කොටස බොහෝ දුරට පළමු දෙකට සේවය කිරීම අරමුණු කර ගෙන ඇත) නමුත් 'පුම්බන ලද' වර්ධනය ප්‍රධාන වශයෙන් ඵලදායි නොවන හෝ ප්‍රමාණවත් නොවන ඵලදායි, යටිතල පහසුකම් සහ දේපල වෙළඳාම් සඳහා ආයෝජන වලින් සමන්විත වේ" (චීනයේ ආර්ථිකය අනුගමනය කළ හැකි එකම මාර්ග පහයි).

භෞතික යටිතල පහසුකම් සංවර්ධනය සහ කොටස් වෙළෙඳපොළ දීර්ඝ කාලයක් ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ වර්ධනයට හා සංවර්ධනයට සමත් විය. සැබෑ මිනිසුන්ට සැබෑ ප්‍රතිලාභ හෝ කල්පවත්නා ආර්ථික දියුණුව සම්බන්ධයෙන් අපට පෙන්වීමට ඇත්තේ ඉතා සුළු ප්‍රමාණයකින්, අපි ණය ගොඩ ගැසුවෙමු.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ දරිද්‍රතා අනුපාතය මේ වන විට 50% ඉක්මවා ඇත. ජෝන් හොප්කින්ස් විශ්ව විද්‍යාලයේ ස්ටීව් හෑන්ක්ස් පෙන්වා දුන් පරිදි, "උද්ධමනය දුප්පත්ම අයව තලා දමයි..." (අල් ජසීරා - 28.5.2022). සේවයෙන් ඉවත් කිරීම විරැකියාව ඉහළ නංවන අතර, මුළු පවුලම දරිද්‍රතාවයට තල්ලු කරනු ඇත. ලිංගික ශ්‍රමිකයින් වෙනුවෙන් පෙනී සිටින කන්ඩායමක් වන Stand Up Movement Lanka (SUML) ට අනුව, "ජනවාරි මාසයේ සිට කොළඹ ලිංගික කර්මාන්තයට සම්බන්ධ වන කාන්තාවන්ගේ සියයට 30 ක වැඩිවීමක් දක්නට ලැබේ" (The Telegraph - 28.5.2022). සංඛ්‍යාව වැඩි කළ හැක්කේ වැඩි කිරීම පමණි. ත්‍රිපෝෂ කම්හල වහලා; ළමා මන්දපෝෂණ මට්ටම් කෙරෙහි එහි බලපෑම විනාශකාරී වනු ඇත. අඩු බරින් උපදින ළදරුවන් වැඩි වනු ඇත, වැඩෙන දරුවන් වැඩි ප්‍රමාණයක්, පාසල් හැර යන සිසුන් සංඛ්‍යාව වැඩි වනු ඇත, ළමා හා මාතෘ මරණ සංඛ්‍යාව වැඩි වනු ඇත, අනාථ වැඩිහිටියන් වැඩි වේ. දශක හතක සමාජ ජයග්‍රහණ අතුගා දමනු ඇත. එක පවුලක අශිෂ්ට බල කුසගින්න නිසා පරම්පරා කිහිපයක් අහිමි වනු ඇත.

නව ආර්ථික ප්‍රතිපත්ති රාමුව සහ අගමැති වික්‍රමසිංහ මහතා පොරොන්දු වී ඇති අයවැය මත බොහෝ දේ රඳා පවතිනු ඇත. රජය දුප්පතුන් කුඩු කිරීම නවත්වා පුද්ගලික සහ ආයතනික ආදායම් බදු වැඩි කර ආදායම වැඩි කර අපගේම 1% ඉලක්ක කර ධන බද්දක් හඳුන්වා දෙයිද? මිලිටරි වියදම් සහ ප්‍රාග්ධන වියදම් කප්පාදු කර එයින් ලැබෙන ඉතුරුම් දුප්පතුන් සාගතයෙන් ආරක්ෂා කිරීමට යොදා ගනීවිද? විදුලිබල හා ඉන්ධන අර්බුද සමනය කිරීමේ මාර්ගයක් ලෙස සූර්ය බලශක්තිය සහ පොදු ප්‍රවාහනය වැඩිදියුණු කිරීම කෙරෙහි අවධානය යොමු කරන්නේද?
එසේත් නැතිනම් හුදු කතාන්දර පමණක් සිදුවනු ඇති අතර පීඩාකාරී ආර්ථික ප්‍රතිපත්ති සහ ප්‍රචණ්ඩ විරෝධතා, රාජ්‍ය ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය සහ මහජන ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය යන විෂම චක්‍රයකට මඟ පෙන්වන ක්‍රියාවක් නොවේද?

උදාහරණ දෙකක්, එකක් අලුත් සහ අනෙක පැරණි. පාවිච්චි කරන ලද ප්ලාස්ටික් බෝතල් එකතු කර ජාත්‍යන්තර ක්‍රීඩා සන්නාම සඳහා ප්‍රතිචක්‍රීකරණය කරන ලද පොලියෙස්ටර් බවට පත් කරන ලාංකීය සමාගමක් වන BPPL Holdings PLC වෙත එක්සත් ජනපද ජාත්‍යන්තර සංවර්ධන මූල්‍ය සංස්ථාවෙන් ඩොලර් මිලියන 15 ක ණය මුදලක් පිරිනමන ලදී. ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ අසාර්ථක රාජ්‍යයට ඩොලර් මිලියනයක ණයක් පවා ලබා ගැනීමට අපහසු වන අතර, නව්‍ය පෞද්ගලික සහ සමාජ ව්‍යවසායන්ට තවමත් ජාත්‍යන්තර ප්‍රදාන සහ ණය ලබා ගත හැකිය.

රණසිංහ ප්‍රේමදාස මහතා පාසල් සිසුන් සඳහා නොමිලේ දහවල් ආහාර වැඩසටහනක් ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීම පිළිබඳ අදහස ඉදිරිපත් කළ අවස්ථාවේදී ජයවර්ධන ජනාධිපතිවරයාගේ කැබිනට් මණ්ඩලය මුදල් නොමැතිකම හේතුවෙන් එය ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළේය. ඇත්තටම රට දේශපාලන-ආර්ථික මඩ ගොහොරුවක තිබුණා. එහෙත් ළමා මන්දපෝෂණය ද උග්‍ර වෙමින් තිබිණි. දරිද්‍රතා රේඛාවට පහළින් හෝ ඊට ආසන්නව 50% ක පමණ ජනතාවක් ජීවත් වන කොළඹ නගරයේ මෙම වැඩසටහන ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීමේ අදහස අගමැති ප්‍රේමදාස සහ නගරාධිපති සිරිසේන කුරේ විසින් ඉදිරිපත් කරන ලදී. අරමුදල් සොයා ගැනීම සඳහා සිරිසේන කුරේ, සෑම වසර පහකට වරක් ගාස්තු වැඩි කිරීමේ CMC හි සාමාන්‍ය පුරුද්ද වෙනුවට වාර්ෂික සහ ඊට වඩා කුඩා අනුපාත වැඩිවීමක් සමඟ විශාල මුදලකින් ප්‍රතිස්ථාපනය කළේය. ප්‍රේමදාස ජනාධිපතිතුමාගේ පාලන සමයේදී ජාතික වශයෙන් ක්‍රියාත්මක වූ නොමිලයේ දවල් ආහාර වැඩසටහනේ පූර්වගාමියා බවට පත්වූයේ මෙම වැඩසටහනයි.

යථාර්ථය පිළිගන්න, නිර්මාණශීලීව සිතන්න, යහපතෙහි සතුරා වීමට වඩා හොඳ වීමට ඉඩ නොදෙන්න. අපට අවශ්‍ය වන්නේ 21 වැනි ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනය, ධනවතුන් මත වැඩි බදු පැනවීම සහ පැරණි සහ නව දුප්පතුන් ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වයට තල්ලු කිරීම නැවැත්වීමට ආරක්ෂිත දැලක් අවශ්‍ය වේ. දෙකම එකට; දෙකම දැන්.

ta.title: நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரம் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி முறை செல்ல வேண்டும். எப்படி என்பது தான் கேள்வி. எதார்த்தமாக, ஒரு இடைக்கால நடவடிக்கையாகவும் புதிய அரசியலமைப்புக்கான பாலமாகவும் சிறந்த 21வது திருத்தத்தை முன்வைப்பதே ஒரே வழி.
body_lines: இலங்கை: 2019, குறைந்த நடுத்தர வருமானம் கொண்ட நாடு; 2020, திவாலான, பிச்சை எடுக்கும் நிலம், உயிர்வாழ்வதற்காக சர்வதேச தொண்டு நிறுவனத்தை சார்ந்துள்ளது.

"ஏழை அரசியல் என் வாழ்க்கையை சிதைத்து விட்டது, என் குழந்தைகளுக்கு உணவை மேசையில் வைப்பது இதுதான் எனது ஒரே நம்பிக்கை." பேச்சாளர் 33 வயதான இலங்கைப் பெண், அவிழ்ந்து வரும் பொருளாதாரத்தால் பாலியல் தொழிலாளியாக மாற வேண்டிய கட்டாயம் ஏற்பட்டது (தி டெலிகிராப் - 28.5.2022).

மோசமான அரசியல், மோசமான பொருளாதாரத்தை விளைவித்தது - அதுவே இலங்கையின் ஒப்பீட்டளவில் வெற்றியிலிருந்து முழுமையான தோல்விக்கு தலைசுற்ற வைக்கும் சரிவை ஏற்படுத்தியது. அண்மையில் நடைபெற்ற COPE கூட்டத்தில், மத்திய வங்கி அதிகாரிகளும் நாணயச் சபை உறுப்பினர்களும் பாரிய வரி குறைப்பு மற்றும் ஒரு டொலருக்கு ரூபாயின் பெறுமதியை 203 ஆக பராமரிக்க பில்லியன் கணக்கான வெளிநாட்டு கையிருப்புகளை வீணடிப்பது பற்றி பேசினர். வெகுஜன பணம் அச்சிடுதல் மற்றும் abracadabra பாணி உரக் கொள்கையைச் சேர்க்கவும், உங்களிடம் இது உள்ளது: நான்கு படிகளில் ஒரு நாட்டை எவ்வாறு அழிப்பது.

நாணயச் சபையின் கலாநிதி ராணி ஜயமஹா, கடனைத் தாங்க முடியாத தன்மையைக் காரணம் காட்டி, அதன் விரைவான நிதியளிப்புக் கருவியின் கீழ் செலுத்தும் சமநிலை ஆதரவிற்காக இலங்கைக்கு கடனை வழங்க சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியம் மறுத்ததாக கோப் நிறுவனத்திடம் தெரிவித்தார். அது 2020 இன் முதல் பாதியில். அந்த எச்சரிக்கை கம்பளத்தின் கீழ் அடித்துச் செல்லப்பட்டது. புளிப்பு திராட்சை கதையில் வரும் நரி போல ராஜபக்சேக்கள், ‘யாருக்கு ஐஎம்எப் வேண்டும்!’ என்று அலறினர்.

ஏன் அந்த பழைய நிலத்திற்கு செல்ல வேண்டும்? வீடு எரிந்தது, எரிந்தது; மோசமான புயல்களில் இருந்து நம்மைக் காத்துக் கொள்ள ஒரு அடிப்படை தங்குமிடம் அமைப்பதில் கவனம் செலுத்த வேண்டும். அதுதான் ராஜபக்சே வாதம். ஆனால் வீடு ஏன் தீப்பிடித்தது என்பதை நாம் புரிந்து கொள்ளாத வரை, புதிய தங்குமிடத்தை இதேபோன்ற பேரழிவிலிருந்து பாதுகாக்க எப்படி நடவடிக்கை எடுக்க முடியும்?

மோசமான அரசியல் மோசமான பொருளாதாரத்தை வளர்க்கிறது. ஒரு குடும்பம் இந்த குழப்பத்தில் இலங்கையை இறக்கியது. பிப்ரவரி 2022 இல், மருந்தக உரிமையாளர்கள் சங்கம் தற்போதைய மருந்து கையிருப்பு மூன்று மாதங்களுக்கு மட்டுமே போதுமானது என்று எச்சரித்தது. அந்த நேரத்தில் மத்திய வங்கி ஏற்கனவே நமது தங்க கையிருப்பில் பாதியை விற்றுவிட்டது. கவனத்தில் கொள்ள முடியாத அளவுக்கு அரசு மும்முரமாக இருந்தது. பெப்ரவரியில், நாமல் ராஜபக்ச கொழும்பு துறைமுக நகரத்தில் நீர் விளையாட்டு மையத்தை திறந்து வைத்தார். அதே மாதத்தில் காலி தர்மபால பூங்காவை ஜனாதிபதி திறந்து வைத்தார். அவரது மூலோபாய நகரங்கள் மேம்பாட்டுத் திட்டத்தின் கீழ் 200 மில்லியன். மார்ச் மாத இறுதியில் கூட, ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய அரச நிறுவனமொன்றுக்கு மீண்டும் விஜயம் செய்ய திட்டமிட்டிருந்தார். மத்திய சுற்றாடல் அதிகார சபையின் ஊழியர்களுக்கு கோவிட்-19 பூஸ்டர் ஷாட்டை எடுத்து ஆன்டிஜென் பரிசோதனைக்கு உட்படுத்துமாறு கடிதம் ஒன்று வழங்கப்பட்டது. ஏப்ரல் முட்டாள்கள் தினத்தில் திட்டமிடப்பட்ட இந்த விஜயம், மிரிஹான எதிர்ப்பினால் மட்டுமே தடுக்கப்பட்டது.

ராஜபக்ச குலத்தை இலங்கையை திவாலாக்க வைத்த அதிகாரக் குவிப்பு முடிவுக்குக் கொண்டுவரப்பட வேண்டும். எந்தவொரு தனிநபரும் (அவரது குடும்பத்துடன் அல்லது இல்லாமலே) மீட்புக்கான நமது பாதையைத் தடுத்து, நம்மை மீண்டும் அழிவுக்குள் தள்ள முடியாது என்பதை நாம் உறுதி செய்ய வேண்டும்.

இதனால் 21வது திருத்தத்தின் முக்கியத்துவம். நல்ல பொருளாதாரம் நல்ல அரசியல் இல்லாமல் இருக்க முடியாது; வருவாயை உயர்த்துவதற்கும், செலவுகளைக் குறைப்பதற்கும், பணவீக்கத்தைக் கட்டுப்படுத்துவதற்கும், பழைய மற்றும் புதிய ஏழைகளுக்கு போதுமான பாதுகாப்பு வலையை உருவாக்குவதற்கும் ஒரு புதிய கொள்கை கட்டமைப்பானது 21வது திருத்தத்துடன் கைகோர்க்க வேண்டும்.

ராஜபக்சக்கள் ஒருபோதும் வெளியேறவில்லை. மக்கள் கோபம் தணியும் வரை அவர்கள் மரவேலைக்குள் பின்வாங்கினர். இப்போது மீண்டும் வந்துவிட்டனர். பசில் ராஜபக்ச எப்பொழுதும் போல் இழுபறியில் ஈடுபட்டுள்ளார். குடும்பத்தின் கைகளில் முறையான அதிகாரத்தை வைத்திருப்பதே அவரது முக்கிய அக்கறை. அதற்கு அவர் 21வது திருத்தத்தை தடுக்க வேண்டும்.

அரசியலை விட பொருளாதாரத்திற்கு முதன்மை வழங்கப்பட வேண்டும் என்ற வாதம், ராஜபக்சவின் அதிகாரத்திற்குச் சேவை செய்யும் வகையில் இட்டுக்கட்டப்பட்ட வாதம். பொருளாதாரத்தை சூறையாடிய ராஜபக்சக்கள் இப்போது அதன் விளைவான வலியை பயன்படுத்தி அதிகாரத்தில் தொங்குகின்றனர். இது ஆபாசமானது ஆனால் 21வது திருத்தத்தை நிறைவேற்ற எதிர்க்கட்சிகள் ஒன்றிணையாவிட்டால் அது வெற்றியடையலாம்.

எங்களிடம் ஒரு ஜனாதிபதி முறை இல்லை என்றால், ஒரு நபரும் அவரது குடும்பமும் பொருளாதாரத்தை இவ்வளவு எளிதாகவும் தண்டனையின்றியும் அகற்ற முடியாது. எங்களிடம் ஜனாதிபதி முறை இல்லை என்றால், பொருளாதாரத்திற்கு தீ வைத்த தலைவர் இப்போது வேலை இல்லாமல் இருப்பார். நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரம் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி முறை செல்ல வேண்டும். எப்படி என்பது தான் கேள்வி. ஜனாதிபதியின் அதிகாரங்களை உடனடியாகக் குறைக்கும் திருத்தத்தை நிராகரித்து புதிய அரசியலமைப்புச் சட்டத்தை முன்வைக்கிறோமா? அல்லது 21 ஆவது திருத்தச் சட்டத்திற்குப் பின் புதிய அரசியலமைப்புச் சட்டம் கொண்டு வரப்படவுள்ள இரண்டு படிநிலை தீர்வுக்கு நாம் செல்வதா?

எதிர்க்கட்சிகள் ஒப்புக்கொள்ள மறுக்கும் உண்மையை ராஜபக்சக்களுக்கு தெரியும். 21வது திருத்தம் விரைவில் மீண்டு வர வேண்டும் என்ற குடும்பத்தின் கனவுக்கு கிட்டத்தட்ட ஆபத்தானது. அதனால்தான் பசில் - மற்றும் அநேகமாக நாமல் - ராஜபக்ச 21 வது திருத்தத்தை தாமதப்படுத்தவும் அழிக்கவும் SLPP யை அணிதிரட்டுகிறார்கள். அவர்கள் வெற்றி பெற்றால், 21வது திருத்தம் அல்லது புதிய அரசியலமைப்பு எதுவும் இருக்காது.

நாடாளுமன்றத் தேர்தல் அவசியமானது, ஆனால் தற்போதைய சூழ்நிலையில் நடைமுறையில் சாத்தியமற்றது. பெரும்பான்மையான மக்கள், பற்றாக்குறையால் பாதிக்கப்பட்டு, பணவீக்கத்தால் நொறுக்கப்பட்ட உயிர்வாழ்வதற்கான அன்றாடப் போரில் சிக்கித் தவிக்கின்றனர். தேர்தல் பிரச்சாரம் என்பது ஐந்து அல்லது அதற்கு மேற்பட்ட வாரங்களுக்கு தேசிய மேடையில் மீண்டும் செயல்படுத்தப்படும் பாராளுமன்ற அமர்வாகும். சாமானிய மக்கள் பசியிலும் தேவையிலும் தவிக்கும் போது, ​​அரசியல்வாதிகள் பற்றாக்குறையான வளங்களை பிரச்சாரத்திற்காக வீணடிக்கிறார்கள். அந்தக் காட்சியானது வாக்களிப்பதற்கான ஆர்வத்தை ஏற்படுத்தாது, மாறாக பாராளுமன்றத்தை உருவகமாகவும், வார்த்தையளவிலும் குலைக்கச் செய்யும். ஒட்டுமொத்த ஜனநாயக அமைப்புக்கு எதிராக பொதுமக்களின் கோபத்தைத் தூண்டும் ஒரு தீப்பொறியாக தேர்தல் செயல்படக்கூடும்.

சிறந்த, உடனடித் தேர்தலில் குறைந்த வாக்குப்பதிவு இருக்கும்; மோசமான நிலையில், அரசியல்வாதிகள் மற்றும் அவர்களின் கூட்டங்களைத் தாக்கும் பசி மற்றும் விரக்தியுள்ள மக்கள் இருப்பார்கள். 20 ஆவது திருத்தச் சட்டத்தின் பிரகாரம் ஜனாதிபதியின் அதிகாரங்கள் குறைக்கப்படாமலேயே தேர்தல் நடத்தப்படவுள்ளமையினால் இவ்வாறானதொரு நிலைமை ராஜபக்சவின் கைகளுக்குச் செல்லும். பொதுமக்களின் கோபத்தின் வெளிப்பாடுகள் அராஜகத்தை எல்லைக்குட்படுத்தினால், அது ராஜபக்சக்களுடன் அல்லது இல்லாவிட்டாலும் இராணுவத் தலையீட்டிற்கான கதவைத் திறக்கலாம்.
எதார்த்தமாக, ஒரு இடைக்கால நடவடிக்கையாகவும் புதிய அரசியலமைப்புக்கான பாலமாகவும் சிறந்த 21வது திருத்தத்தை முன்வைப்பதே ஒரே வழி.

இலங்கையின் பிச்சை ராஜபக்சேக்களின் புனிதத்தன்மையை நீக்கி விட்டது. மகிந்தவும், கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்சவும் தங்கள் ஒளிவட்டத்தை இழந்துள்ளனர். குடும்பம் பலவீனமானது, அரசியல் ரீதியாக பிளவுபட்டிருக்கலாம். மே 9 நிகழ்வுகள் வரை, வெவ்வேறு ராஜபக்சக்களின் பல்வேறு அபிலாஷைகளில் சில சமச்சீரற்ற தன்மை இருந்தது. இப்போது அவர்களின் மாறுபட்ட ஆர்வங்களுக்கு இடையே சமச்சீர் இல்லை.

SLPP மீதான பசில் ராஜபக்சவின் பிடியை குறைமதிப்பிற்கு உட்படுத்துவதற்கு எதிர்க்கட்சி இந்த நலன்களின் வேறுபாட்டைப் பயன்படுத்திக் கொள்ளலாம், அதன் மூலம் பாராளுமன்றத்தில் ராஜபக்ஷ அதிகாரம் (துணை சபாநாயகர் தேர்தலின் போது அந்த அதிகாரம் காட்டப்பட்டது). சரியாக கையாண்டால், 21வது திருத்தம் SLPP யை பிளவுபடுத்த பயன்படுகிறது, இன்னும் சில முதுகெலும்புகள் உள்ளவர்கள் பசிலுக்கும் நாமல் ராஜபக்சவிற்கும் வாழ்நாள் முழுவதும் அடிமையாக இருந்து தங்களை காப்பாற்றிக் கொள்ள ஒரு வழியை வழங்குகிறது.

ஸ்ரீலங்கா பொதுஜன பெரமுன பிளவுபட்டால், அது கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷவை தம்மை மட்டுமே கவனிக்க வைக்கும். எல்லாவற்றிற்கும் மேலாக, அவரது சிறந்த நலன்கள், அவரது குடும்பத்தில் இருந்து வேறுபட்டது, 21 வது திருத்தத்தை ஊக்குவிப்பதிலும், ஜனாதிபதி பதவியை ஒழிப்பதை ஆதரிப்பதிலும் உள்ளது. ராஜபக்ச குடும்பத்திற்கு இழப்பதற்கு ஒன்றுமில்லை, மீளப் பெறுவதற்கு உலகமும் இல்லை. கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ச இழக்க வேண்டிய ஒன்று உள்ளது, ஜனாதிபதி பதவியில் இருந்து ஒழுங்கான விலகல். இறுதியாக நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரம் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி முறையை இல்லாதொழித்த நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரம் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி என்ற ரீதியில் அவருக்கும் ஏதோ ஒன்று கிடைத்துள்ளது.

காலி முகத்திடலில் முன்னணி ஆர்ப்பாட்டக்காரர்களின் சமீபத்திய வருந்தத்தக்க கைதுகள் புதுப்பிக்கப்பட்ட அடக்குமுறையின் ஆபத்தை சுட்டிக்காட்டுகின்றன. 20வது திருத்தம் பொலிஸை மீண்டும் அரசியலாக்கியது, SLPP நாடாளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர்கள் அவர்கள் விரும்பும் பகுதிக்கு OIC களை நியமிக்கும் வகையில், சமீபத்தில் தெரியவந்தது. இத்தகைய அமைப்பு குறிப்பாக ஒரு புதிய அடக்குமுறை அலைக்கு உகந்தது. 21வது திருத்தம் பொலிஸ் அதிகாரங்களை துஷ்பிரயோகம் செய்வதற்கும் நீதித்துறையை மேலும் பலப்படுத்துவதற்கும் தடையாக அமையும். 21வது திருத்தத்தின் கீழ் நடத்தப்படும் தேர்தல் மிகவும் சுதந்திரமாகவும் நியாயமாகவும் இருக்கும், இதனால் அனைத்து எதிர்க்கட்சிகளும் பயனடையும். தற்செயலாக, அவர் ஒரு தேர்தலையும் ஜனாதிபதி பதவிக்கான வாக்கெடுப்பையும் ஒன்றாக நடத்தலாம். சமீபத்திய CPA கருத்துக் கணிப்பின்படி, பெரும்பாலான இலங்கையர்கள் ஜனாதிபதி பதவியை ஒழிப்பதற்கு வாக்களிப்பார்கள். வரவிருக்கும் பாராளுமன்றம் தன்னை அரசியலமைப்பு சபையாக மாற்றி புதிய ஜனநாயக அரசியலமைப்பை உருவாக்க முடியும்.

20 ஆம் நூற்றாண்டின் பாடங்கள் குறித்த தனது கருத்துக்களில், இத்தாலிய வரலாற்றாசிரியரும் பாசிச எதிர்ப்புப் போராளியுமான லியோ வாலியானி, “நீதியின் இலட்சியங்களின் வெற்றி என்பது சமத்துவம் என்பது எப்பொழுதும் இடைக்காலமானது என்பதை நமது நூற்றாண்டு நிரூபிக்கிறது, ஆனால் சுதந்திரத்தைப் பாதுகாத்தால், நாம் எப்போதுமே மீண்டும் தொடங்கலாம்…” (தி ஏஜ் ஆஃப் எக்ஸ்ட்ரீம்ஸில் மேற்கோள் காட்டப்பட்டுள்ளது - எரிக் ஹோப்ஸ்வாம்). 21வது திருத்தம் இலங்கை ஜனநாயகத்தை ராஜபக்ச அதிகார அபகரிப்பு மற்றும் இராணுவ தலையீடு ஆகிய இரண்டிலிருந்தும் காப்பாற்றுவதற்கான இன்றியமையாத முதல் படியாகும்.

ஜே.வி.பி குறைந்தபட்சம் 21வது திருத்தத்தை ஆதரிப்பதற்கான நிபந்தனைகளை முன்வைத்துள்ளது. SJB மற்றும் பிற எதிர்க்கட்சிகள் இன்னும் அதை செய்யவில்லை. 21வது திருத்தத்திற்கான போராட்டம் எதிர்க்கட்சிகள் தோற்கடிக்கப்பட வேண்டும். எதிர்கட்சிகள் இலட்சியத்தை நிலைநிறுத்தி, அதன் மூலம் சாத்தியமானதைத் தடுத்து, மிக மோசமானதை வெற்றிகரமான முறையில் திரும்பச் செய்ய முடியுமா?

யதார்த்தத்தை எதிர்கொள்வோம். இந்தியா இல்லாமல், சில வாரங்களுக்கு முன்பே இலங்கை ஸ்தம்பித்திருக்கும். நாங்கள் கோரும் புதிய கடனை இந்தியா எங்களுக்கு வழங்கவில்லை என்றால், நாங்கள் இன்னும் நின்றுவிடலாம். இந்திய எதிர்ப்பு அரசியலை அரவணைக்கும் நேரம் இதுவல்ல. இது நாகரீகமற்றது மற்றும் சுய அழிவுகரமான முட்டாள்தனமானது. இந்தியா கைகழுவிவிட்டால், கச்சத்தீவையோ, திருக்கோயில் எண்ணெய் தொட்டி பண்ணையையோ சாப்பிடலாமா?

சீனாவிடம் இருந்து இதே போன்ற உதவிகள் சாத்தியமில்லை, ஏனெனில் சீனா தன்னைத்தானே நீட்டிக்கொண்டது. பெய்ஜிங்கின் புதிய கவனம் பசிபிக் மற்றும் இந்தியப் பெருங்கடல் அல்ல. மேற்கு நாடுகள் உக்ரைனில் கவனம் செலுத்துகின்றன. இந்தியாவும் ஓரளவுக்கு ஜப்பானும் மட்டுமே எங்களின் ஒரே நம்பிக்கை.

சீனாவின் சொந்த பொருளாதார பிரச்சனைகளில் இருந்து நாம் கற்றுக்கொள்ளக்கூடிய ஒன்று உள்ளது. பெய்ஜிங் பல்கலைக்கழகத்தின் பேராசிரியரான மைக்கேல் பெட்டிஸின் கூற்றுப்படி, உண்மையான மற்றும் உயர்த்தப்பட்ட GDP வளர்ச்சிக்கு இடையேயான வேறுபாடு குறித்து ஜனாதிபதி Xi சமீபத்தில் எச்சரித்துள்ளார்: "பரந்த அளவில் பேசினால், உண்மையான வளர்ச்சியானது நுகர்வு, ஏற்றுமதி மற்றும் வணிக முதலீடு ஆகியவற்றால் உருவாக்கப்படும் நிலையான வளர்ச்சி என்று கருதலாம். இந்தக் கூறுகளில் கடைசியானது பெரும்பாலும் முதல் இரண்டிற்குச் சேவை செய்வதை நோக்கமாகக் கொண்டது) அதேசமயம் 'உயர்த்தப்பட்ட' வளர்ச்சியானது முக்கியமாக உற்பத்தி அல்லாத அல்லது போதுமான அளவு உற்பத்தி செய்யாதது, உள்கட்டமைப்பு மற்றும் ரியல் எஸ்டேட்டில் முதலீடு" (சீனாவின் பொருளாதாரம் பின்பற்றக்கூடிய ஐந்து பாதைகள் மட்டுமே).

பௌதீக உட்கட்டமைப்பு அபிவிருத்தி மற்றும் பங்குச் சந்தை என்பன நீண்ட காலமாக இலங்கையின் வளர்ச்சி மற்றும் அபிவிருத்திக்காக கடந்து வந்தன. உண்மையான மக்களுக்கு உண்மையான நன்மைகள் அல்லது நீடித்த பொருளாதார முன்னேற்றம் ஆகியவற்றின் அடிப்படையில் நாங்கள் கடனைக் குவித்துள்ளோம்.

இலங்கையின் வறுமை விகிதம் தற்போது 50% க்கும் அதிகமாக உள்ளது. ஜான் ஹாப்கின்ஸ் பல்கலைக்கழகத்தின் ஸ்டீவ் ஹாங்க்ஸ் சுட்டிக்காட்டியபடி, "பணவீக்கம் ஏழைகளை நசுக்குகிறது..." (அல் ஜசீரா - 28.5.2022). ஆட்குறைப்பு வேலையில்லா திண்டாட்டத்தை அதிகரிக்கும், முழு குடும்பத்தையும் வறுமையில் தள்ளும். ஸ்டாண்ட் அப் மூவ்மென்ட் லங்கா (SUML) என்ற பாலியல் தொழிலாளர்களுக்கான வழக்கறிஞர் குழுவின் படி, "ஜனவரி முதல் கொழும்பில் பாலியல் துறையில் சேரும் பெண்களின் எண்ணிக்கை 30 சதவீதம் அதிகரித்துள்ளது" (தி டெலிகிராப் - 28.5.2022). எண்ணிக்கை அதிகரிக்கத்தான் முடியும். திரிபோஷ தொழிற்சாலை மூடப்பட்டுள்ளது; குழந்தைகளின் ஊட்டச்சத்து குறைபாட்டின் மீது அதன் தாக்கம் பேரழிவை ஏற்படுத்தும். அதிக எடையுடன் பிறக்கும் குழந்தைகள், வளர்ச்சி குன்றிய குழந்தைகள், படிப்பை பாதியில் நிறுத்தும் மாணவர்கள், குழந்தைகள் மற்றும் தாய் இறப்புகள், ஆதரவற்ற முதியவர்கள் அதிகம். ஏழு தசாப்தங்களின் சமூக சாதனைகள் அழிக்கப்படும். பல தலைமுறைகள் இழக்கப்படும், ஒரு குடும்பத்தின் மோசமான அதிகாரப் பசிக்கு நன்றி.

புதிய பொருளாதாரக் கொள்கை கட்டமைப்பு மற்றும் வரவு செலவுத் திட்டத்தில் பிரதமர் விக்கிரமசிங்க உறுதியளித்திருப்பதன் அடிப்படையில்தான் அதிகம் தங்கியுள்ளது. அரசாங்கம் ஏழைகளை தூள்தூளாக்குவதை நிறுத்திவிட்டு, தனிநபர் மற்றும் பெருநிறுவன வருமான வரிகளை அதிகரித்து வருவாயை உயர்த்தி, நமது சொந்த 1% இலக்கை இலக்காகக் கொண்டு செல்வ வரியை அறிமுகப்படுத்துமா? இராணுவச் செலவும் மூலதனச் செலவும் குறைக்கப்பட்டு, அதனால் கிடைக்கும் சேமிப்பு ஏழைகளை பட்டினியில் இருந்து பாதுகாக்கப் பயன்படுத்தப்படுமா? மின்சாரம் மற்றும் எரிபொருள் நெருக்கடியைப் போக்க சூரிய சக்தி மற்றும் பொது போக்குவரத்தை மேம்படுத்துவதில் கவனம் செலுத்தப்படுமா?
அல்லது வெறும் பேச்சாக இருக்குமா, எந்த நடவடிக்கையும் அடக்குமுறை பொருளாதாரக் கொள்கைகள் மற்றும் வன்முறைப் போராட்டங்கள், அரசு வன்முறை மற்றும் மக்கள் வன்முறை ஆகியவற்றின் தீய சுழற்சிக்கு வழிவகுக்கும்?

இரண்டு எடுத்துக்காட்டுகள், ஒன்று புதியது மற்றொன்று பழையது. BPPL Holdings PLC, பயன்படுத்தப்பட்ட பிளாஸ்டிக் போத்தல்களை சேகரித்து, சர்வதேச விளையாட்டு வர்த்தக முத்திரைகளுக்கு மறுசுழற்சி செய்யப்பட்ட பாலியஸ்டராக மாற்றும் இலங்கை நிறுவனத்திற்கு, யுனைடெட் ஸ்டேட்ஸ் இன்டர்நேஷனல் டெவலப்மெண்ட் ஃபைனான்ஸ் கார்ப்பரேஷனிடமிருந்து $15 மில்லியன் கடனாக வழங்கப்பட்டது. இலங்கையின் தோல்வியடைந்த அரசு ஒரு மில்லியன் டொலர் கடனைக் கூட பெறுவதற்கு கடினமாக உள்ள நிலையில், புதுமையான தனியார் மற்றும் சமூக நிறுவனங்கள் சர்வதேச மானியங்கள் மற்றும் கடன்களை இன்னும் அணுக முடியும்.

ரணசிங்க பிரேமதாச பாடசாலை மாணவர்களுக்கான இலவச மதிய உணவு திட்டத்தை முன்வைத்த போது ஜனாதிபதி ஜயவர்தனவின் அமைச்சரவை நிதி பற்றாக்குறை காரணமாக அதனை நிராகரித்தது. நாடு உண்மையில் அரசியல்-பொருளாதாரச் சேற்றில் இருந்தது. ஆனால் குழந்தைகளின் ஊட்டச்சத்தின்மையும் தலை விரித்தாடுகிறது. பிரதமர் பிரேமதாச மற்றும் மேயர் சிறிசேன குரே ஆகியோர் கொழும்பில் இந்த திட்டத்தை நடைமுறைப்படுத்துவதற்கான யோசனையை முன்வைத்தனர், அங்கு சுமார் 50% மக்கள் வறுமைக் கோட்டிற்கு கீழே அல்லது அதற்கு அருகில் வாழ்ந்தனர். நிதியைக் கண்டுபிடிப்பதற்காக, சி.எம்.சி.யின் வழக்கமான நடைமுறையான சி.எம்.சி.க்கு ஒவ்வொரு ஐந்து வருடங்களுக்கும் ஒரு பெரிய தொகையை ஆண்டு மற்றும் மிகக் குறைவான கட்டண அதிகரிப்புடன் சிறிசேன குரே மாற்றினார். பிரேமதாச ஜனாதிபதியாக இருந்த காலத்தில் தேசிய ரீதியில் நடைமுறைப்படுத்தப்பட்ட இலவச மதிய உணவு திட்டத்தின் முன்னோடியாக இந்த வேலைத்திட்டம் அமைந்தது.

யதார்த்தத்தை ஏற்றுக்கொள்ளுங்கள், ஆக்கப்பூர்வமாக சிந்தியுங்கள், மேலும் நல்லதுக்கு எதிரியாக இருக்க விடாதீர்கள். 21வது திருத்தம், பணக்காரர்கள் மீது அதிக வரிகள், பழைய மற்றும் புதிய ஏழைகள் வன்முறைக்கு தள்ளப்படுவதைத் தடுக்கும் பாதுகாப்பு வலை ஆகியவை நமக்குத் தேவை. இரண்டும் ஒன்றாக; இரண்டும் இப்போது.

en.title: The executive presidency must go. The only question is how. Realistically, the only option is to push for the best possible 21st Amendment as an interim measure and as a bridge to a new constitution.
body_lines: Sri Lanka: 2019, a lower middle income country; 2020, a bankrupted, beggared land, dependent on international charity for survival.

“Poor politics has shattered my life and this is my only hope to put food on the table for my children.” The speaker is a 33-year-old Sri Lankan woman who was forced by the unravelling economy to become a sex worker (The Telegraph – 28.5.2022).

Poor politics resulting in bad economics – that was what caused Sri Lanka’s dizzying slide from relative success to absolute failure. At a recent COPE meeting, Central Bank officials and Monetary Board members talked of the massive tax cut and the wasting of billions in foreign reserves to maintain the rupee at 203 to a dollar. Add mass money printing and an abracadabra-style fertiliser policy and you have it: How to ruin a country in four steps.

Dr. Ranee Jayamaha of the Monetary Board informed COPE that the IMF refused to give Sri Lanka a loan for balance of payment support under its Rapid Financing Instrument citing debt unsustainability. That was in the first half of 2020. That warning was swept under the carpet. The Rajapaksas, like the fox in the sour grapes story, cried, ‘Who needs the IMF!’

Why go over that old ground? The house is burnt, gutted; we should focus on putting up a basic shelter to shield ourselves from the worst of the storms. That is the Rajapaksa argument. But unless we understand why the house caught fire, how can we take measures to protect the new shelter from a similar calamity?

Bad politics breed bad economics. One family landed Sri Lanka in this mess. In February 2022, the pharmacy owners association warned that current medicine stocks were enough only for three months. By that time the Central Bank has already sold half of our gold reserves. The Government was too busy to take note. In February, Namal Rajapaksa inaugurated a water sports centre at the Colombo Port City “offering a range of exhilarating sea-borne experiences…aimed at filling an unmet need.” In the same month the President inaugurated the Dharmapala Park in Galle, renovated at Rs. 200 million, under his Strategic Cities Development Program. Even as late as end March, President Gotabaya was planning another visit to a State institution. A letter was issued to the staff of the Central Environment Authority telling them to get the COVID-19 booster shot and undergo an antigen test. The visit, scheduled for April Fool’s Day, was prevented only by the Mirihana protest.

The concentration of power which enabled the Rajapaksa clan to bankrupt Sri Lanka must be ended. We must ensure that no one individual (with or without his family) can block our path to recovery and hurl us into ruin again.

Thus the importance of the 21st Amendment. Good economics cannot be without good politics; a new policy framework to raise revenue, cut costs, curb inflations, create an adequate safety net for the old and new poor must go hand in hand with the 21st Amendment.

The Rajapaksas never left. They merely retreated into the woodwork until public anger subsided. Now they are back. Basil Rajapaksa is as busy as ever, pulling strings. His main concern is to keep formal power in the hands of the family. To do that, he must prevent the 21st Amendment.

The argument that economics must be given primacy over politics is a specious one, concocted in service of Rajapaksa power. The Rajapaksas who gutted the economy are now using the resultant pain to hang on to power. It is obscene but it may succeed, if the Opposition doesn’t get together to push the 21st Amendment through.

If we did not have a presidential system, one man and his family would not have been able to gut the economy with such ease and impunity. If we didn’t have a presidential system, the leader who set fire to the economy would be out of a job now. The executive presidency must go. The only question is how. Do we reject an amendment to reduce presidential powers immediately and hold out for a new constitution? Or do we go for a two-step solution, a 21st Amendment to be followed by a new constitution?

The Rajapaksas know the truth the Opposition is refusing to acknowledge. A 21st Amendment is near-fatal to the family’s dream of making a comeback soon. That is why Basil – and probably Namal – Rajapaksa is rallying the SLPP to delay and destroy the 21st Amendment. If they succeed, there’ll be neither a 21st Amendment nor a new constitution.

A parliamentary election is necessary, but practically impossible in current conditions. The absolute majority of people are caught up in the daily battle for survival, battered by shortages, pulverised by inflation. An election campaign will be a parliamentary session re-enacted on a national stage for five or more weeks. While ordinary people suffer in hunger and want, politicians will be wasting scarce resources on propaganda. That spectacle is likely to cause not an eagerness to vote, but to gut the parliament, metaphorically and literally. An election might act as a spark to ignite public anger against the entire democratic system.

At best, an immediate election will have a low turnout; at worst we will have hungry and despairing people attacking politicians and their meetings. Such a situation will play into Rajapaksa hands since the election will be held with the 20th Amendment extant and presidential powers untrimmed. If expressions of public ire border on anarchy, it might even open the door to military intervention with or without the Rajapaksas.
Realistically, the only option is to push for the best possible 21st Amendment as an interim measure and as a bridge to a new constitution.

The beggaring of Sri Lanka has caused the de-sanctification of the Rajapaksas. Mahinda and Gotabaya Rajapaksa have lost their halos. The family is weak, and possibly politically divided. Until the events of 9 May, there was certain symmetry in the varying ambitions of different Rajapaksas, giving them an objective reason to work together as a family. Now there is no symmetry between their varying interests.

The Opposition can use this divergence of interests to undermine Basil Rajapaksa’s hold on the SLPP, and through that Rajapaksa power in Parliament (that power was on display during the election for a deputy speaker). If handled correctly, the 21st Amendment can be used to split the SLPP, giving a way out for those who still have some form of vertebrae to save themselves from a lifetime of slavering for Basil and Namal Rajapaksa.

If the SLPP splits, that might persuade Gotabaya Rajapaksa to look out solely for himself. After all, his best interests, as distinct from his family’s, lie in promoting the 21st Amendment and backing the abolition of the presidency. The Rajapaksa family has nothing to lose and a world to regain. Gotabaya Rajapaksa has something to lose, an orderly departure from presidency. He also has something to gain, a grain of redemption as the executive president who finally abolished the executive presidency.

The recent deplorable arrests of leading Galle Face protestors point to the danger of renewed repression. The 20th Amendment re-politicised the police, enabling SLPP parliamentarians to appoint the area OICs of their choice, as was recently revealed. Such a system is particularly conducive to a new wave of repression. A 21st Amendment will act as a bar to the abuse of police powers and further strengthen the judiciary. An election held under the 21st Amendment will be more free and fair, and thus of benefit to all Opposition parties. Incidentally, an election and a referendum on the presidency can he held together. Going by the latest CPA opinion poll, most Lankans would vote to abolish the presidency. The incoming parliament can then turn itself into a constitutional council and draft a new democratic constitution.

In his comments on the lessons of 20th Century, Italian historian and anti-fascist fighter Leo Valiani said, “Our century demonstrates that the victory of the ideals of justice is equality is always ephemeral, but also that, if we manage to preserve liberty, we can always start all over again…” (quoted in The Age of Extremes – Eric Hobsbwam). The 21st Amendment is the essential first step in saving Lankan democracy both from a Rajapaksa power grab and a military intervention.

The JVP has at least come up with their conditions for supporting the 21st Amendment. The SJB and other Opposition parties are yet to do even that. The battle for the 21st Amendment is for Opposition’s to lose. Will the Opposition hold out for the ideal, thereby preventing the possible and enabling the very worst to make a triumphant return?

Let’s face reality. Without India, Sri Lanka would have come to a standstill literally weeks ago. If India doesn’t grant us the new loan we are seeking, we may come to a standstill yet. This is not the time to embrace politics of anti-Indianism. It is impolite, and self-destructively stupid. If India washes her hands off us, can we eat Kachchativu or the Trinco oil tank farm?

Similar help from China is unlikely for China is scaling back, having overstretched itself. Pacific and not the Indian Ocean is Beijing’s new focus. The West is focused on Ukraine. India and to a lesser extent Japan are our only hope.

There is something we can learn from China’s own economic troubles. According to Michael Pettis, a professor at Beijing University, President Xi has recently warned about the difference between genuine and inflated GDP growth: “Broadly speaking, genuine growth can be thought of as sustainable growth generated largely by consumption, exports, and business investment (with the last of these elements aimed mostly at serving the first two) whereas ‘inflated’ growth consist mainly of non-productive or insufficiently productive, investment in infrastructure and real estate” (The only five paths China’s economy can follow).

Physical infrastructure development and stock market were what passed for growth and development in Sri Lanka for a long time. We piled up debt, with very little to show for it in terms of actual benefits to real people or lasting economic improvement.

Sri Lanka’s poverty rate is probably over the 50% by now. As Steve Hanks of John Hopkins University pointed out, “Inflation is crushing the poorest…” (Al Jazeera – 28.5.2022). Retrenchment will drive up unemployment, pushing entire families into poverty. According to the Stand Up Movement Lanka (SUML), an advocacy group for sex workers, “There has been a 30 per cent increase in women joining the sex industry in Colombo since January” (The Telegraph – 28.5.2022). The numbers can only increase. The Thriposha factory has been closed; its impact on child malnutrition levels will be devastating. There will be more babies born underweight, more children growing stunted, more students dropping out of school, more child and maternal deaths, more destitute elderly. Social achievements of seven decades will be wiped out. Several generations will be lost, thanks to the imbecilic power hunger of one family.

Much will depend on the new economic policy framework and the budget Prime Minister Wickremesinghe has promised. Will the Government stop pulverising the poor and raise revenue by increasing personal and corporate income taxes and introducing a wealth tax targeting our own 1%? Will military expenditure and capital expenditure be pruned and resultant savings used to protect the poor from starvation? Will there be focus on solar power and on improving public transport, as a way of alleviating the power and the fuel crises?
Or will there be just talk, and no action leading to a vicious cycle of oppressive economic policies and violent protests, State violence and popular violence?

Two examples, one new and the other old. BPPL Holdings PLC, a Lankan company which collects used plastic bottles and turns them into recycled polyester for international sports brands, was awarded a $ 15 million loan from the United States International Development Finance Corporation. While Sri Lanka’s failed state is hard pressed to obtain even a loan of a million dollars, innovative private and social enterprises can still access international grants and loans.

When Ranasinghe Premadasa came up with the idea for a free midday meal program for schoolchildren, the cabinet of President Jayewardene turned it down due to lack of funds. The country was indeed in a politico-economic mire. But child malnutrition too was becoming rampant. PM Premadasa and Mayor Sirisena Cooray came up with the idea of implementing the program in Colombo, where around 50% of the people lived below or close to the poverty line. To find the funds, Sirisena Cooray replaced the CMC’s usual practice of increasing rates every five years by a large sum with an annual and much smaller rate increase. This program became the precursor of the free midday meal program implemented nationally during the Premadasa presidency.

Accept reality, think creatively, and don’t let better be the enemy of good. We need the 21st Amendment, higher taxes on the rich, and a safety net to stop the old and new poor from being driven to violence. Both together; both now.
